attacking_style: Pure sprinter who launches from 150-200m in chaotic finishes, often winning from deep positions (6th-7th wheel) using superior raw speed rather than leadout organization.
team_tactics: Frequently operates without dedicated leadout, stealing rival trains (ACKERMANN, MERLIER, RICHEZE wheels documented) rather than relying on own team; adaptable to disorganized finishes.
terrain_preference: Excels in chaotic flat sprints and technical uphill drags up to 4%; severely exposed on moderate climbs, crosswinds, and sustained high-tempo headwind efforts.
vulnerability: Crashes and positioning errors in technical finales (barrier pinch, wheel contact documented 5+ times); crosswind splits; dropped on even moderate climbs 76km+ from finish; physical fragility pattern (abandons, crashes).
key_patterns: Wins from far back (6th/7th position at 200m) using exceptional late acceleration, 4+ documented instances
Frequently boxed or poorly positioned despite speed advantage; barriers/crashes in sprints observed 6+ times
Dropped in crosswinds and moderate climbs; pure sprinter threshold very low, 4+ observations
Steals rival leadouts rather than using own team; ACKERMANN/MERLIER/RICHEZE wheels documented pattern
Physical fragility: abandoned Giro 2021 with knee pain, Tour 2021 broken collarbone, multiple crashes
Form peaked 2019-2021 with 3 Tour stage wins debut year; 2022-2023 runner-up pattern suggests declining top-end speed
form_trajectory: negative: Multiple 2nd places in 2022-2023 Tour after three years without win; marginally slower than PHILIPSEN despite regaining sprint positioning, suggesting peak speed decline from 2019-2021 dominance
